reality.name: VLESS + REALITY
reality.description:
Advanced proxy with TLS camouflage.
No domain needed. Very fast.
Hides traffic as normal HTTPS.

wizard_steps.help: This will install Xray-core and configure a REALITY proxy that disguises your traffic as normal HTTPS. No domain name is needed. Port 443 must be available.
Your server will impersonate this website. Censors will see what looks like traffic to this site. All options work well.

wizard_steps.prompt: Use a domain instead of IP in client links?
wizard_steps.help: If you have a domain pointing to this server (Cloudflare proxy OFF), you can use it in connection links. Otherwise your server IP will be used.
Create an A record pointing to your server IP. Make sure Cloudflare proxy is OFF (gray cloud). Leave empty to use server IP.
This will be the first user who can connect. You can add more users later from the management menu.

wg.name: WireGuard
wg.description:
Fast VPN tunnel with native apps.
Full device tunnel. Simple setup.
Best for daily use.

wizard_steps.help: This will install WireGuard and create a VPN tunnel. All device traffic will be routed through your server. UDP port 51820 must be open.
This will be the first VPN user. A config file and QR code will be generated for easy mobile setup.

ws.name: VLESS + WS + CDN
ws.description:
Route through Cloudflare CDN.
Hides your server IP completely.
Requires a domain name.

wizard_steps.help: This routes traffic through Cloudflare's CDN, hiding your server IP. You need a domain name on Cloudflare (free plan works). Set Cloudflare SSL mode to 'Flexible'.
Your domain must be on Cloudflare with the proxy enabled (orange cloud). SSL mode in Cloudflare must be set to 'Flexible'. The domain should resolve to your server IP.
This will be the first user. Connection links will use your Cloudflare-proxied domain.

mtp.name: MTProto Proxy
mtp.description:
Telegram-specific proxy.
Built into Telegram apps.
No extra client needed.

clients.note: Built into Telegram apps. Just click the proxy link!

wizard_steps.help: This installs a Telegram-specific proxy. Users won't need any extra apps - they just click a link in Telegram to connect.
Port 443 is recommended (looks like HTTPS). If 443 is in use, try 8443. Any port works but 443 is least likely to be blocked.
Fake-TLS makes traffic look like real HTTPS. Secure mode uses random padding. Fake-TLS is recommended for most cases.
When using Fake-TLS mode, traffic will look like HTTPS to this domain. Use a popular website for best results.
A proxy link will be generated that users can click in Telegram to start using the proxy.

dnstt.name: DNS Tunnel
dnstt.description:
Emergency DNS tunnel.
Works during total blackouts.
Very slow but unblockable.

clients.note: Requires dnstt-client binary. See docs for setup.

wizard_steps.help: DNSTT encodes traffic inside DNS queries. This is an EMERGENCY protocol - very slow (~50 KB/s) but nearly impossible to block. Use only when everything else fails.

wizard_steps.prompt: This protocol is very slow. Continue?
wizard_steps.help: DNSTT is designed for emergency situations where all other protocols are blocked. Normal browsing will be very slow. Consider installing Reality or WireGuard for daily use.
You need a domain where you can set DNS records. Required: 1) A record for ns1.domain -> your server IP. 2) NS record for t.domain -> ns1.domain. The domain must NOT be behind Cloudflare proxy.

conduit.name: Conduit (Psiphon)
conduit.description:
Psiphon volunteer relay.
Help users in censored regions.
No client configuration needed.

clients.note: No client needed. Users connect via Psiphon apps.

wizard_steps.help: This turns your server into a volunteer relay for the Psiphon network. You'll be helping users in censored regions access the internet. Docker will be installed if not present.
How many simultaneous users can relay through your server. More clients = more bandwidth used. Recommended: 200-1000.
Set a bandwidth cap to control costs. -1 means no limit. Set based on your VM's bandwidth allowance.

vray.name: VLESS + TLS
vray.description:
Classic V2Ray setup with TLS.
Requires domain + certificate.
Good compatibility.

wizard_steps.help: This is a classic V2Ray setup with real TLS certificates from Let's Encrypt. Requires a domain name pointing to your server.
Your domain must have an A record pointing to this server. A free TLS certificate will be obtained from Let's Encrypt.
Connection links will use your domain with proper TLS encryption.

sos.name: SOS Emergency Chat
sos.description:
Encrypted emergency chat.
Works over DNS tunnel.
No internet needed.

wizard_steps.help: This installs the SOS relay daemon for encrypted emergency chat. Messages are encrypted end-to-end and travel through DNS tunnels. Requires DNSTT to be running first.

hysteria.name: Hysteria v2
hysteria.description:
QUIC-based high-speed proxy.
Best on lossy/throttled networks.
Uses UDP.

wizard_steps.help: Hysteria v2 uses QUIC (UDP) with aggressive congestion control. Fastest protocol on lossy networks. Won't work if UDP is fully blocked.
Choose a UDP port. Default 8443 works well. Make sure UDP is not blocked on this port by your cloud provider firewall.

wizard_steps.prompt: Use a domain for TLS certificate?
wizard_steps.help: If you have a domain, Hysteria can get a real certificate via ACME. Otherwise it uses a self-signed cert (clients must allow insecure).
Domain must have an A record pointing to this server. A free TLS cert will be obtained automatically.
First user for Hysteria v2. Connection config will be generated.

http-obfs.name: HTTP Obfuscation
http-obfs.description:
CDN host header spoofing.
Hides behind popular domains.
Requires domain + CDN.
requirements: Client must have clean CDN IPs

wizard_steps.help: This sets up VLESS+WS behind Cloudflare CDN with Host header spoofing. The server side is identical to WS+CDN. The difference is on the client: users connect to 'clean' CDN IPs instead of the domain. Use the cfray scanner to find clean IPs.
Your domain on Cloudflare with proxy enabled (orange cloud). SSL mode must be 'Flexible'. This is the same setup as WS+CDN.
Connection links will include your domain as the Host header. Client must set a clean CDN IP as the address.

ssh-tunnel.name: SSH Tunnel
ssh-tunnel.description:
Basic SOCKS5 proxy over SSH.
Zero install. Universal.
Easily detected by DPI.

clients.note: Built into every OS: ssh -D 1080 user@server

wizard_steps.help: Creates a SOCKS5 proxy through SSH. No additional software needed on server. Client connects with: ssh -D 1080 user@server. Easily blocked by DPI - use as fallback only.
A restricted user will be created for SSH tunnel access. This user can only create tunnels, not get a shell.

slipstream.name: Slipstream
slipstream.description:
Enhanced DNS tunnel with QUIC.
~63 KB/s throughput.
Emergency protocol.

wizard_steps.help: Slipstream is an enhanced DNS tunnel with QUIC+TLS. Faster than DNSTT (~63 KB/s vs ~42 KB/s). Uses port 53 - cannot run alongside DNSTT or NoizDNS.

wizard_steps.prompt: This is a slow emergency protocol. Continue?
wizard_steps.help: Slipstream is faster than DNSTT but still slow. Suitable for messaging & light browsing, not streaming. Use Reality/WS for daily use.
Set up NS record: ns1.domain -> server IP, t.domain NS -> ns1.domain. Domain must NOT be behind Cloudflare proxy.

noizdns.name: NoizDNS
noizdns.description:
DPI-resistant DNS tunnel.
DNSTT fork with noise/padding.
Emergency protocol.

wizard_steps.help: NoizDNS is a DPI-resistant fork of DNSTT that adds noise and padding to DNS queries. Uses port 53 - cannot run alongside DNSTT or Slipstream.

wizard_steps.prompt: This is a slow emergency protocol. Continue?
wizard_steps.help: NoizDNS throughput is similar to DNSTT (~42 KB/s) but with better DPI resistance. Use for emergency access only.
Set up NS record: ns1.domain -> server IP, t.domain NS -> ns1.domain. Domain must NOT be behind Cloudflare proxy.

tor-bridge.name: Tor Bridge (obfs4)
tor-bridge.description:
obfs4 pluggable transport.
Help Tor users bypass censorship.
Volunteer relay.

clients.note: Tor users connect via BridgeDB. No manual setup.

wizard_steps.help: This runs an obfs4 Tor bridge on your server. Censored users can discover it through BridgeDB and connect to the Tor network via your server.
The port Tor clients connect to. Default 9001 is standard. Make sure this port is open in your firewall.
Optional contact info for BridgeDB. Can be left empty. Used by Tor project to contact you about your bridge.

snowflake.name: Snowflake Proxy
snowflake.description:
WebRTC Tor relay.
Zero config needed.
Minimal resource usage.

clients.note: Tor users connect automatically. No manual setup.

wizard_steps.help: This runs a Snowflake WebRTC relay. Censored Tor users connect through your relay automatically. Zero configuration needed - just start the container.
